brief.title: Finance & Banking Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Monitor portfolio performance and risk exposure
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal finance & banking systems. Updated weekly. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Operations Team Leads in the Finance & Banking sector. The primary goal is to monitor portfolio performance and risk exposure. Data is refreshed weekly, and users have intermediate data literacy. The design prioritizes clarity and actionability.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: Bullet Chart is well-suited for 'Monitor trend over time' with Transaction Volume.
Funnel Chart is well-suited for 'Compare across categories' with Fraud Detection Rate.
Bar Chart is well-suited for 'Track against target' with Cost-to-Income Ratio.
Area Chart is well-suited for 'Identify outliers' with Capital Adequacy Ratio.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Single-page scrollable: Summary -> Trend -> Breakdown -> Details.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Transaction Volume, Fraud Detection Rate
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Transaction Volume over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Dark theme: Charcoal (#1E1E1E) with green (#00FF88) for positive, red (#FF4444) for negative
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: K/M suffix for large numbers; 1 decimal for percentages
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone

primary_interactions: Cross-filtering: clicking one chart filters all others
interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For intermediate users: balance simplicity with analytical depth.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With weekly updates, include a prominent 'last updated' timestamp.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts selected based on primary user task: trend -> line chart; comparison -> bar chart; part-to-whole -> pie/donut; single value -> KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen. Group related KPIs with whitespace and section headers.

brief.title: Retail Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Monitor store sales performance by region
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal retail systems. Updated weekly. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Data Analysts and BI Developers in the Retail sector. The primary goal is to monitor store sales performance by region. Data is refreshed weekly, and users have intermediate data literacy. The design prioritizes clarity and actionability.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: Treemap is well-suited for 'Monitor trend over time' with Sales per Square Meter.
Funnel Chart is well-suited for 'Compare across categories' with Inventory Shrinkage.
Heatmap is well-suited for 'Track against target' with Gross Margin.
Bar Chart is well-suited for 'Identify outliers' with Customer Foot Traffic.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Top row: 3-4 KPI scorecards. Middle: 2 main charts side-by-side. Bottom: detail table.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Sales per Square Meter, Inventory Shrinkage
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Sales per Square Meter over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Neutral Grey background with teal accents (#008080) and amber warnings (#FFA500)
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: K/M suffix for large numbers; 1 decimal for percentages
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone

interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For intermediate users: balance simplicity with analytical depth.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With weekly updates, include a prominent 'last updated' timestamp.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts selected based on primary user task: trend -> line chart; comparison -> bar chart; part-to-whole -> pie/donut; single value -> KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen. Group related KPIs with whitespace and section headers.

layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Daily Active Users over time

brief.title: E-Commerce Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Monitor daily revenue and conversion rates
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal e-commerce systems. Updated daily. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Sales Managers and Regional Directors in the E-Commerce sector. The primary goal is to monitor daily revenue and conversion rates. Data is refreshed daily, and users have beginner data literacy. The design prioritizes clarity and actionability.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: Area Chart is well-suited for 'Monitor trend over time' with Revenue.
Bar Chart is well-suited for 'Compare across categories' with Conversion Rate.
Bullet Chart is well-suited for 'Track against target' with Average Order Value.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Grid layout: 12-column responsive. Hero metric top-left, supporting charts fill remaining space.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Revenue, Conversion Rate
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Revenue over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Healthcare White with calming blue (#4A90D9) and green (#27AE60) for good metrics
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: K/M suffix for large numbers; 1 decimal for percentages
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone

interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For beginner users: use simple KPI cards and avoid complex charts.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With daily updates, include a prominent 'last updated' timestamp.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts selected based on primary user task: trend -> line chart; comparison -> bar chart; part-to-whole -> pie/donut; single value -> KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen. Group related KPIs with whitespace and section headers.
